channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Thousands march in anti-Trump protests just days before former president returns to power
item.description: Demonstrators rallied in St. Paul on Saturday afternoon, braving single-digit temperatures to protest ahead of President-elect Donald Trump inauguration. Trump takes the oath of office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Feven Gerezgiher and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons: Here’s what to know about potential clemencies
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to make good on his promises to pardon at least some Jan. 6 rioters on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: HHS cuts off funding for EcoHealth Alliance in light of COVID Committee evidence
item.description: HHS formally debarred EcoHealth Alliance Inc. and its former president Dr. Peter Daszak for allegedly failing to report dangerous gain-of-function research in China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Liberal California may have a political 'reawakening' after wildfire disaster, historian predicts
item.description: Historian Victor Davis Hanson predicts an impending cultural shift in California, as the fires in Los Angeles have impacted liberal residents who may be changing their tune on politics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Biden wraps up half-century political career, his failure to turn page on Trump weighs on president
item.description: President Biden, whose half-century political career comes to a close on Monday as Donald Trump is inaugurated and succeeds him in the White House, leaves office as one of the nation's most unpopular presidents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Free upgrades and pardon requests: What it's like to share a name with a president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There’s not only one Donald Trump in the United States. Nor is there only one William J. Clinton. Plenty of people share names with famous presidents. Donald Trump, a veteran and resident of Alabama, said because of his name he often is gifted things for free. William J.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams, President-elect Trump meet in Florida; Adams says they didn't discuss his legal case
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams met with President-elect Trump in Palm Beach, Florida, on Friday, ahead of Trump's inauguration on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House reveals Biden's final list of clemency for nearly 2,5000 people, setting new presidential record
item.description: President Biden's final list of commutations was released Friday afternoon, laying out the names and registration numbers of nearly 2,500 inmates whose sentences were reduced by President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tampa mayor meets with other U.S. mayors, responds to proposed conditions on disaster aid
item.description: Tampa Mayor Jane Castor was in Washington D.C. this weekend for the U.S. Conference of Mayors’ winter meeting. It's a time when hundreds of city leaders unite to discuss challenges and solutions involving affordable housing, infrastructure, immigration, and the climate.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden sets record by commuting sentences of nearly 2,500 people convicted on nonviolent drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden announced Friday that he was commuting the sentences of almost 2,500 people convicted of nonviolent drug offenses, using his final days in office on a flurry of clemency actions meant to nullify prison terms he deemed too harsh.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's swearing-in will move inside the Capitol Rotunda because of intense cold weather
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump will take the oath of office from inside the Capitol Rotunda on Monday due to forecasts of intense cold weather, upending months of meticulous planning for a massive outdoor event with crowds sprawling down the National Mall.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CBO projects US debt to grow $23.9 trillion in 10 years, not including costs of extending tax cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The national debt is slated to rise by $23.9 trillion over the next decade, a sum that does not include trillions of dollars in additional tax cuts being championed by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court will hear case of Maryland parents who object to LGBTQ books in their kids' classes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday agreed to hear an appeal from parents in suburban Washington who want to pull their children from elementary school classes that use books featuring LGBTQ characters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mel Gibson 'surprised' to be named Hollywood envoy
item.description: Mel Gibson said he was stunned to be appointed a special envoy to Hollywood under the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: List: Tech moguls attending Trump's inauguration
item.description: The heads of five of the top eight largest companies (by market cap) reportedly plan to attend his inauguration on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Biden sets record by commuting sentences of nearly 2,500 people convicted on nonviolent drug charges
item.description: President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of almost 2,500 people convicted of nonviolent drug offenses. Friday’s clemency actions give Biden the presidential record for most individual pardons and commutations issued.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump taps Voight, Gibson and Stallone as 'special envoys' to Hollywood
item.description: "These three very talented people will be my eyes and ears, and I will get done what they suggest. It will again be, like The United States of America itself, The Golden Age of Hollywood!" Trump posted.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Ratcliffe edges closer to CIA confirmation with Democratic support: 5 takeaways
item.description: John Ratcliffe emerged largely unscathed from a hearing before the Senate Intelligence Committee that appeared to pave the way for him to serve as the next director of the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA).
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Senate adds amendment to immigration bill named after Laken Riley
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday adopted the first amendment to the Laken Riley Act, as Republicans push for a legislative win to open the new Congress. The legislation in its current form would mandate federal detention of immigrants without legal status accused of theft, burglary and other related crimes.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Fetterman's approach to Trump stirs speculation
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman’s (D-Pa.) open-minded approach to President-elect Trump is fueling questions about his motives and vexing some of his fellow Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's pick for secretary of the Interior Department is Doug Burgum. Here's what to know
item.description: Doug Burgum, former North Dakota governor and businessman is President Donald Trump's pick to be Secretary of the Interior. The confirmation hearing is scheduled for Thurs., Jan 16, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert, MPR News

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tampa Bay native Pam Bondi goes through Senate confirmation hearings to become U.S. Attorney General
item.description: Pam Bondi grew up in Temple Terrace, she worked in the Hillsborough County State Attorney's office as a prosecutor, and she served two terms as the elected Florida State Attorney General.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gov. Murphy calling for cellphone ban in NJ classrooms
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (PIX11) - Could there be a day when there will be no cellphones in New Jersey classrooms? In his State of the State address on Tuesday, Gov. Phil Murphy announced his support for legislation that would direct school districts to adopt policies that ban cellphones in K-12 classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: End to daylight saving time among bills for 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: A Republican lawmaker who represents parts of six rural Nevada counties is sponsoring a bill to take the state off daylight saving time — one of the 200 bills written so far.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about the special election for Vargas' Board of Supervisors seat
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors on Tuesday finalized plans to fill the seat formerly held by Nora Vargas before her abrupt exit from elected office through a special election.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Few US adults confident Justice Department and FBI will act fairly under Trump, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: As President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi, prepares for questioning from senators on Wednesday, only about 2 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's Cabinet pick for attorney general is Pam Bondi. Here's what to know
item.description: Bondi is expected to receive broad support from Republicans but face sharp questions from Democrats who have concerns about whether she will protect the Justice Department from political influence.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota legislative standoff spills out of the Statehouse into the courthouse
item.description: With accusations flying over which party is ignoring state law, the fight to control the Minnesota House is shifting from lawmakers to lawyers. DFL Secretary of State Steve Simon is calling on the Minnesota Supreme Court to weigh in on who has what power.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: County Board of Supervisors votes to hold special election to fill District 1 seat
item.description: In a swift and unanimous vote, the County Board of Supervisors has agreed to a special election to fill the vacancy left behind by Nora Vargas.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Vara, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin’s governor creates new violence-prevention office in wake of school shooting
item.description: Wisconsin’s Democratic governor on Tuesday created a new office dedicated to preventing gun violence, a month after a school shooting not far from the state Capitol and an idea that drew immediate opposition from Republicans who said it was misguided.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hegseth confronts allegations of misconduct as senators grill Trump’s choice for Pentagon chief
item.description: Minnesotan Pete Hegseth was combative at times, as he was forced to confront the allegations of misconduct and his own comments that are far from the military mainstream.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge: DFL candidate’s election stands for Shakopee-area Minnesota House seat
item.description: State Rep. Brad Tabke, a DFL Minnesota House incumbent who narrowly won reelection, should be able to begin a new term, a judge ruled Tuesday — despite a GOP election challenge centering on a pool of ballots that were cast but never counted.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's inauguration weekend features fireworks, MAGA rally, inaugural balls
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration weekend will start with a fireworks display outside his northern Virginia golf club and end with a trio of inaugural balls where he will speak, the committee planning the events announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
